title: Sensible Trade Overhaul
description: Revamps villager trades in fair, balanced manner.
body:
Since the release of version 1.17 the trade has remained untouched, aside few minor additions. The concept of making the trade system more complex is a fine addition to the game, however the offers itself shifted balance dramatically, it became almost mandatory to set master tiers for certain villager profession (smiths for diamond tools and armour, librarian for enchantments, farmer for golden carrots) as it is most efficient way to complete the equipment in game now.

Solution is to turn the trade to more of a auxiliary than main source of income, give more purpose to other professions and establish more stable and intuitive economy.

## Technical details:

**This datapack requires [VillagerConfig](https://modrinth.com/mod/villagerconfig) to work properly.**

It can be freely added, updated or removed from Minecraft save, however you may need to refresh trade list for each villager. "Locked" trades will not be changed.

Despite Config being built on Fabric you can launch it on Forge via [Connector](https://modrinth.com/mod/connector).


### **Summary**

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

- Removed Diamond tier from weapon and armour offers;
- Repurposed professions to reward players with convienent, yet fair trades;
- Modded trading system resembles "lesser effort, lesser reward";
- Introduced renewable source of important resources (e.g. sand, iron, diamonds, sponge).

</details>


### **Trade Stock**

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

- Tools, enchanted books and rare items can be bought in much lesser amount (varying between 3-8) in comparision to regular items (16-32 cycles);
- Enchanted weaponry and armoury can be bought only once per stock;
- The stock amount can be disabled via [VillagerConfig](https://modrinth.com/mod/villagerconfig).

</details>


### **Proficiency leveling**

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

- Villager with limited trade offers (e.g. Cartographer, Smiths, Fisherman) require less trades to level up;
- Costly trade offers provide more experience to the villager, to give a reason for choosing them over cheaper offers;
- Generally faster leveling process.

</details>
